channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Romy Ash’s 'Mantle' Explores Humanity, Nature, and an Unusual Fungal Pandemic
item.description: Romy Ash’s latest eco-fiction novel, 'Mantle', explores a near-future fungal pandemic that intertwines the natural world with human experience in unexpectedly beautiful ways. Set in southern Tasmania,...

item.title: Sydney Swans dominate AFL with speed, handball chains and standout forward performance
item.description: Sydney Swans surged to the top of the AFL ladder following a thrilling 19.17 (131) to 17.12 (114) win over Melbourne, powered by Malcolm Rosas Jr’s seven-goal haul. Rosas, grandson of WAFL legend Bill...

item.title: Government Moves to Compensate One Constitution Avenue Apartment Owners After Lease Dispute
item.description: ISLAMABAD: The federal government has decided to compensate apartment owners of One Constitution Avenue at the price they originally paid. The move comes after a prolonged legal and administrative dis...

item.title: Sir Alex Ferguson Taken to Hospital After Feeling Unwell at Old Trafford
item.description: Legendary former Manchester United manager, Sir Alex Ferguson, waka go hospital on May 3, 2026, after he fall sick for Old Trafford just small time before the big match wey suppose happen between Manc...

item.title: Director Agnieszka Holland Announces Biopic on Franz Kafka with Limited Ticket Giveaway
item.description: Award-winning director Agnieszka Holland is embarking on what is described as her most ambitious project yet: a biographical film about Franz Kafka, the renowned 20th-century Czech writer known for hi...

item.title: Vote Counting Underway in Key Indian States with Modi’s Party Seeking Gains
item.description: Vote counting is ongoing in several important Indian states, with special attention on West Bengal, where Prime Minister Narendra Modi’s Bharatiya Janata Party (BJP) is trying to make significant gain...

item.title: UAE Leaves Opec Amid Output and Geopolitical Disagreements
item.description: The United Arab Emirates, one of the largest oil producers in Opec, officially left the organisation after nearly sixty years of membership, causing a major disruption in global oil dynamics. The UAE ...

item.title: Lauren Sanchez and Jeff Bezos Attend Pre-Met Gala in New York City
item.description: Jeff Bezos and Lauren Sanchez were seen arriving at Anna Wintour's pre-Met Gala event in New York City on Sunday evening. The Amazon founder, aged 62, looked polished in a light grey suit, black shirt...

item.title: Sandra Mbuvi Honoured as Top Female Entrepreneur at Africa Golden Awards
item.description: Former Nairobi governor Mike Sonko is beaming with pride after his daughter, Sandra Mbuvi, was recognised as the African Top Best Female Entrepreneur at the Africa Golden Awards. The event, which took...

item.title: Chelsea to Face Nottingham Forest at Early Monday Kick-Off Due to Europa League Clash
item.description: Chelsea are set to play Nottingham Forest on Monday at 5 pm EAT, an unusual time for a Premier League weekday match. Initially, the game was supposed to take place on Saturday, May 2, but Nottingham F...

item.title: Sassa and Postbank Disagree on Grant Service Fees Amid Contract Dispute
item.description: The South African Social Security Agency (Sassa) and Postbank are currently in a dispute over a long-standing agreement that made Postbank the primary disburser of social grants. Postbank has claimed ...

item.title: House Justice Committee to Approve Impeachment Report Against VP Sara Duterte
item.description: MANILA, Philippines – The House Justice Committee is set to reconvene on Monday, May 4, to vote on a committee report consolidating the impeachment case against Vice President Sara Duterte. The commit...

item.title: Saskatoon Hosts Its First Aviation Day for Children on the Autism Spectrum
item.description: On Saturday morning, families gathered at the Saskatoon John G. Diefenbaker International Airport for the city’s first Autism Aviation Day. This initiative, hosted by Air Canada in partnership with Au...

item.title: Floods Kill 18 Across Kenya as Tana River and Eastern Region Face Highest Risk
item.description: The number of people who have died due to ongoing floods in Kenya has reached 18, according to the Ministry of Interior and National Administration. Most of these deaths have been caused by drowning a...

item.title: Suspected Hantavirus Outbreak Aboard Netherlands Cruise Ship Claims Three Lives
item.description: Three passengers aboard the Netherlands-based cruise ship MV Hondius have died, and three others are reported ill due to a suspected hantavirus outbreak. The ship, operated by Oceanwide Expeditions, w...

item.title: Russia’s latest drone and missile strikes kill 10 across Ukraine
item.description: Russian strikes have killed at least 10 people and injured 76 across several Ukrainian cities, according to officials. The attacks targeted Kherson, Odesa, Donetsk, Zaporizhzhia, and Sumy regions. In ...

item.title: Pakistan and China Enhance Cooperation in Economy, Industry, and Defence
item.description: President Asif Zardari’s week-long visit to China marks a strategic effort by Pakistan to focus its long-standing partnership with China on concrete economic and industrial priorities. The trip emphas...

item.title: Migrants in Australia face long delays and barriers in accessing mental health care, report finds
item.description: A new report from Mental Health Australia highlights significant delays and barriers faced by migrants and culturally diverse communities when seeking mental health support. The research, conducted th...

item.title: Tanzania Strengthens Regional Economic Ties with Kagame and Ruto Visits
item.description: Dar es Salaam — Tanzania is stepping up as a key hub for East African economic integration following back-to-back visits by President Paul Kagame of Rwanda and President William Ruto of Kenya. Analyst...

item.title: ACT public school staff overwhelmingly vote for strike action amid stalled negotiations
item.description: Teachers and school assistants across ACT public schools have voted overwhelmingly in favour of strike action following prolonged negotiations with the Education Directorate. Over 95 per cent of staff...
